summary: Synced the Docker Postgres bootstrap SQL with the soft-delete migration and confirmed the current codebase has no other repository read paths that still need active-row filtering.
reason: The Docker initialization SQL was still using the pre-soft-delete schema, so local container bootstrap needed to match the updated migration and audit behavior from task 03.
risk: Low. This is a synchronization change, but Docker bootstraps would diverge from runtime migrations if left unchanged.
tests: python -c "from pathlib import Path; a=Path(r'D:\coding\lamar-kerja\ayo\backend-sport-team-report-go\internal\platform\database\migrations\001_initial.sql').read_text(); b=Path(r'D:\coding\lamar-kerja\ayo\backend-sport-team-report-go\deployments\docker\postgres\init\001_initial.sql').read_text(); print('MATCH' if a==b else 'DIFF')"
grep scan for existing repository SELECT reads under internal/
notes: Current internal Go code only has one repository read path touching soft-deletable tables, and it already filters `deleted_at IS NULL`.
Future modules should keep treating `deleted_at IS NULL` as the active-row read contract for users, companies, images, teams, players, schedules, and reports.
